periods.objective: Write logarithmic expressions in exponential form and write exponential expressions in logarithmic form.
periods.workedExamples: 6-1, Examples 1-4 — Logarithmic to Exponential Form; Exponential to Logarithmic Form; Evaluate Logarithmic Expressions; Find Inverses of Exponential Functions
periods.notes: Form conversion and inverse-relationship groundwork.

dailyPhases.warmUp: Activate prior knowledge for 6a with one retrieval prompt tied to 6-1.
dailyPhases.conceptDevelopment: Use 6-1, Examples 1-4 — Logarithmic to Exponential Form; Exponential to Logarithmic Form; Evaluate Logarithmic Expressions; Find Inverses of Exponential Functions to develop: Write logarithmic expressions in exponential form and write exponential expressions in logarithmic form..
dailyPhases.guidedPractice: Students complete a scaffolded problem modeled on 6-1, Examples 1-4 — Logarithmic to Exponential Form; Exponential to Logarithmic Form; Evaluate Logarithmic Expressions; Find Inverses of Exponential Functions; teacher checks notation and reasoning.
dailyPhases.independentPractice: Students complete aligned ALEKS/SRS practice: ALEKS M6-L6-1.01, ALEKS M6-L6-1.02.
dailyPhases.assessment: Exit ticket: one fresh problem assessing 6a and one short explanation prompt.
dailyPhases.capReflection: CAP reflection: Name the strategy that helped you persist with write logarithmic expressions in exponential form and write exponential expressions in logarithmic form..
